title: Chest Pain During Dialysis
description: Mechanism-first ACS differential in a dialysis patient.

learningObjectives: Use ECG clues in dialysis patients
Separate demand ischemia from STEMI mimics

card.stem: A 64-year-old man with end-stage renal disease on hemodialysis develops sudden substernal chest pressure 20 minutes into treatment. He is diaphoretic and nauseated. ECG shows peaked T waves with 1 mm ST elevation in V2-V3. Potassium is 6.8 mEq/L.
card.question: What is the most appropriate immediate management?
card.answer: Give IV calcium immediately while evaluating for concurrent acute coronary syndrome.
card.explanation: Severe hyperkalemia can create pseudo-infarction patterns, but membrane stabilization cannot wait. Calcium gluconate reduces the risk of malignant arrhythmia within minutes; insulin-dextrose and dialysis address potassium removal. Ongoing ischemia should still be assessed in parallel because dialysis patients can have real ACS.
teachingPoints: Treat unstable hyperkalemia before diagnostic perfectionism
Dialysis patients may have mixed pathology
Serial ECGs after potassium correction are high yield
card.nextBestStep: IV calcium gluconate, telemetry, repeat ECG after potassium-shifting therapy
card.mechanism: Elevated extracellular potassium lowers the resting membrane potential and impairs cardiac conduction.

title: Brachial Plexus Quick Drill
description: Tap the posterior cord branch before reveal.

learningObjectives: Localize branches of the posterior cord

card.prompt: Identify the branch of the posterior cord that innervates the deltoid.

hotspots.label: Axillary nerve
hotspots.explanation: The axillary nerve travels through the quadrangular space and wraps around the surgical neck of the humerus.

hotspots.label: Radial nerve
hotspots.explanation: The radial nerve is the major terminal branch of the posterior cord supplying the posterior compartments.

card.answerExplanation: The axillary nerve is the posterior cord branch classically injured with surgical neck fractures, producing deltoid weakness and sensory loss over the lateral shoulder.

card.stem: A 9-year-old boy develops cola-colored urine, periorbital edema, and hypertension two weeks after impetigo. Which pathologic process best explains his presentation?

options.label: A
options.text: Subepithelial immune complex deposition after streptococcal infection
options.explanation: Correct. Poststreptococcal glomerulonephritis produces nephritic syndrome with granular immune complex deposition.

options.label: B
options.text: Podocyte effacement caused by T-cell cytokines
options.explanation: This describes minimal change disease, which causes nephrotic syndrome rather than a nephritic picture.

options.label: C
options.explanation: This points to Goodpasture disease, usually with pulmonary hemorrhage and RPGN.

options.label: D
options.text: Mesangial IgA deposition after a mucosal infection
options.explanation: IgA nephropathy follows infections more closely, often within days, not after a latent interval.

card.explanation: The delayed onset after streptococcal skin infection and nephritic findings strongly favor poststreptococcal glomerulonephritis with subepithelial humps.

title: Vocabulary Sprint: Koilonychia
description: Rapid reinforcement of physical exam terminology.

card.advancedDefinition: A concave nail deformity classically associated with chronic iron deficiency and occasionally systemic illness.
card.exampleUsage: On exam, brittle spoon-shaped nails supported chronic iron deficiency as the cause of fatigue.
card.mnemonic: A spoon helps scoop iron-rich cereal.

title: Creator Pearl: ABG Pattern Shortcut
description: Short creator reel on respiratory compensation logic.

card.caption: If the CO2 shift looks too small for the bicarbonate change, stop and ask whether there is a second process hiding in the gas.
card.cta: Save for your next acid-base block
card.educationalTakeaway: Compensation that overshoots or undershoots the expected range suggests a mixed disorder.
